arts.ttl: Everton 'reach agreement' for Alcaraz loan deal
arts.sum: Everton have "reached an agreement" to sign midfielder Carlos Alcaraz on a loan deal from Flamengo, according to the Brazilian club.

arts.ttl: Everton have been in negotiations ‘for months’ to sign player – Unlikely this widow, but one to keep eye on
arts.sum: Everton have been in negotiations ‘for months’ over Eintracht Frankfurt’s Junior Dina Ebimbe, although a move this month is unlikely. That’s according to BILD, who report on the player as he heads back to the German club after failing a medical at Monaco. arts.ttl: Everton transfer agreement quickly wrapped up on Thursday – Easy clauses make permanent move likely
arts.sum: Everton will be obligated to buy Charly Alcaraz from Flamengo when a loan deal for him ends in the summer. That’s according to Meiahora, who report that Alcaraz will leave Flamengo for Everton in the coming days. Reports yesterday indicated that Everton had completed a loan move for the Flamengo midfielder, with the Brazilian club The post Everton transfer agreement quickly wrapped up on Thursday – Easy clauses make permanent move likely appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: Everton vs Leicester – Predicted lineup and team news
arts.sum: Everton host Leicester City in a crucial showdown at Goodison Park this afternoon. David Moyes has galvanised the Toffees since his return to the club earlier this month. Back-to-back wins for the first time this season have lifted Everton seven points clear of the relegation places. arts.ttl: David Moyes had no remorse after touchline bust-up as Everton sent transfer reminder
arts.sum: David Moyes' Everton defeated Manchester City 1-0 on January 31, 2012, with the game being held up by a pitch-invading protester

arts.ttl: Two Leicester City youngsters seal loan transfers
arts.sum: Leicester City right-back Brandon Cover is heading back to League Two after sealing a loan switch to Fleetwood Town. Cover impressed at Port Vale in the first half of the season, but with injured players returning for the Staffordshire club, the 21-year-old’s game-time looked set to reduce in the second half of the campaign.
